results.title: Cooking of France: an alpine menu
results.creator_credit: Hal Weiner, Marilyn Weiner and Screenscope Productions
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “World of Cooking”.
A film which demonstrates that fine French cuisine can be prepared simply, but elegantly, using basic skills and ingredients. Join the amiable Master Chef, Andre Revest, on a tour of his kitchen and his native Dauphiny Alps region.

results.title: Subtle as a Serpent
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Survival Anglia Wildlife Series”.
Depicted as a symbol of evil since the time of Adam and Eve, the snake is one of the most feared creatures on earth. But it is also a creature of great beauty. Lethal and frightening it may be but the snake is also versatile, intelligent and fascinating. Shot in the exotic wilds of Arizona, Kenya, the Amazon Basin and the Great Barrier Reef, the snake is filmed here in its natural habitat. From the safety of the other side of the screen, you can take a close look at the most venomous snakes in the world, and watch a giant anaconda constricting a four-foot cayman. Other specialy created scenes show how a snake can eat a meal five times as big as its own head and how some snakes use infra-red heat detectors to locate their prey.

results.title: Italy: a Venetian menu
results.creator_credit: Hal Weiner, Marilyn Weiner and Screenscope Productions
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “World of Cooking”.
In Venice eating is an event, and master chef Giovanni Giero shows you why. With great aplomb he prepares sauces, scampi, pasta and more. Filmed in and around the beautiful city of Venice.

results.title: The Flight of the Snow Geese
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Survival Anglia Wildlife Series”.
This award winning film tells the story of 300,000 wild snow geese - and the fourteen snowgeese who thought they were human beings. One late spring, naturalist cameramen, Des and Jen Bartlett, marooned themselves on the rugged Arctic tundra for four months. Their objective, to cover for the first time the incredible migration story of the snow geese, from their arctic nesting grounds west of Hudson Bay, south to Texas and the Gulf of Mexico, (a 2,500 mile journey). Living in their tent among the geese, the Bartletts found themselves “leaders” of their own gaggle of strongly attached young geese who now had to make the journey with them. This beautiful film reveals the adventures of the wild geese and the orphans who eventually had to break with their human “parents” and find their way back into the wild. Rated: G

results.title: Balloon safari = Safari by balloon
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Survival Anglia Wildlife Series”.
Shows cameraman, Alan Root, and his wife Jean’s experiences with their own hot air balloon over Kenya. As they were learning how to operate it, forced landings in lakes, thick bush and herds of animals became almost daily occurrences. The Root’s balloon adventures took them across some of the most spectacular places in Africa, but they were drawn towards Mount Kilimanjaro, in Tanzania, where they realised their ambition to fly by balloon above its snow-capped peak.

results.title: Spain: a Catalonian menu
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “World of Cooking”.
People who live in areas with large Latin communities may be familiar with Spanish cooking. But the charming chef Carlos Sentis adds his personal touch and keen insights to the food of northern Spain. Includes the preparation of such Catalonian specialties as Sapa de Pescado and Chicken and Lobster Subur style.

results.title: India: a northern menu
results.creator_credit: Hal Weiner, Marilyn Weiner and Screenscope
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “World of Cooking”.
Just as India is a rich blend of cultures, Indian cooking is a blend of exotic spices. Chef Simon Fernandes and his assistants unveil some aspects of their ancient cuisine.

results.title: Weather forecast
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “World Is Ours”.
International cooperation in weather forecasting. Radio communications system controlled by the World Meteorological Organisation of the United Nations. Numerous examples of how weather forecasting can reduce or eliminate the danger and damage of hurricanes, gales and high tides. Weather studies in the reclamation of desert areas.

results.title: Towards the full life
results.creator_credit: Anthony de Lotbinière, Norman Swallow and BBC-TV
results.brief_description:
Episode of “The World is ours” series
A UNESCO experiment in the Nile Valley where in a few years, by improving agricultural methods and hygiene, and encouraging education, enormous advances have been made in the living standards of Egyptian villagers. A centre was established in the Nile delta for technical and general education which attracted students from all over the Middle East and became the nerve centre of great changes.
